capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an authentication service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise authentication service exists

questions.prompt: Is the authentication service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a session management service or program governing network and application sessions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise session management service exists

questions.prompt: Is the session management service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether session management is reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a credential management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether an enterprise credential management service exists

questions.prompt: Is the credential management service reviewed at least quarterly to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document KPIs and KRIs for the authentication service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication KPIs and KRIs are documented

questions.prompt: Are authentication KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs are reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Do KPIs or KRIs track MFA coverage, phishing-resistant authentication adoption (for example passkeys/FIDO2), or passwordless rollout progress?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether modern authentication adoption metrics are tracked
questions.evaluation_focus: MFA and phishing-resistant authentication coverage metrics

questions.prompt: Do KPIs or KRIs track failed authentication rates, credential stuffing indicators, or suspicious login patterns?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether credential attack and failed auth metrics are tracked
questions.evaluation_focus: Failed authentication and credential attack metrics

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document KPIs and KRIs for session management?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented session management KPIs

questions.prompt: Are session management KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular session KPI review

questions.prompt: Do KPIs measure SSO and federation coverage across applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate SSO coverage metrics

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formal system that authenticates subjects to network and applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal authentication system exists for network and applications
questions.evaluation_focus: Formal authentication system for network and applications

questions.prompt: Is the same authentication system used consistently across the enterprise (VPN, LAN, cloud, and SaaS)—or do separate locations or systems maintain siloed authentication?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication is consistent enterprise-wide

questions.prompt: Does the authentication system support cloud identity providers and federated authentication (for example SAML, OIDC) for enterprise applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether cloud IdP and federation are supported
questions.evaluation_focus: Cloud identity provider and federation support

questions.prompt: Is authentication system configuration reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication configuration is reviewed on a regular cadence

questions.prompt: Does the authentication system integrate with access control policy decision points (PDPs) and policy enforcement points (PEPs) for contextual, identity-centric access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication integrates with access control mechanisms
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration with access control decision and enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an IAM engineer or dedicated authentication owner to configure authentication policies?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a dedicated authentication owner exists
questions.evaluation_focus: IAM engineer or authentication owner for policy configuration

questions.prompt: If authentication is siloed across locations, is there central ownership of authentication policy—or do separate locations have independent authentication administrators?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication engineering is centrally managed

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a dedicated credential management custodian who manages all credentials (passwords, passkeys, certificates)—rather than the IAM engineer who manages the directory alone?
questions.intent: Evaluate dedicated credential custodian role

questions.prompt: Is separation of duties maintained between the creator of the user, the authenticator of the user, and the creator of the credential?
questions.intent: Evaluate separation of duties across identity, authentication, and credential roles
questions.evaluation_focus: Separation of duties for user, authentication, and credential roles

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formally require MFA as a second factor for all access, including LAN, VPN, cloud, application access, and privileged access?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether MFA is required enterprise-wide for all access types

questions.prompt: Are passwordless authentication methodologies (for example FIDO2 passkeys, certificates, or biometrics) documented and deployed enterprise-wide or in defined scopes?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether passwordless authentication is documented and deployed

questions.prompt: Is phishing-resistant MFA (for example FIDO2 security keys or passkeys) documented and deployed enterprise-wide or for high-risk users and applications?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether phishing-resistant MFA is documented and deployed

questions.prompt: Are MFA, passwordless, and phishing-resistant authentication configurations reviewed at least quarterly for improvement?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether modern authentication methods are reviewed on a regular cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Regular MFA and passwordless configuration review

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized documented password and passkey policy for applications, networks, and endpoint devices?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented password and passkey policy
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented password and passkey policy for applications, networks, and endpoints

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized maximum age or validity period for certificates issued?
questions.intent: Evaluate certificate age policy

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized documented policy on how credentials are stored (for example vault, HSM, or encrypted store)?
questions.intent: Evaluate credential storage policy

questions.prompt: Does the organization have a documented risk-based authentication policy that adjusts authentication requirements based on context?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a risk-based authentication policy exists

questions.prompt: Can the authentication system require step-up authentication (stronger factors or re-authentication) for high-risk sessions or sensitive resources?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether adaptive step-up authentication is supported
questions.evaluation_focus: Adaptive step-up authentication for high-risk sessions

questions.prompt: Are device trust, geolocation, and behavioral anomaly signals integrated into authentication decisions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether device and behavioral signals inform authentication
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration of device and behavioral signals into auth decisions

questions.prompt: Are active sessions validated continuously or on a defined interval—not only at initial login?
questions.intent: Evaluate continuous active session validation
questions.evaluation_focus: Continuous or periodic active session validation

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise require step-up re-authentication during sessions when risk signals change (for example new device, location, or sensitive action)?
questions.intent: Evaluate risk-based step-up during sessions

questions.prompt: Are sessions revoked when device posture or security context changes (for example compliance failure or jailbreak detection)?
questions.intent: Evaluate session revocation on context change
questions.evaluation_focus: Session revocation on device posture or context change

questions.prompt: Are token binding or session fixation protections implemented for web and API sessions?
questions.intent: Evaluate token binding and session fixation protections
questions.evaluation_focus: Token binding or session fixation protections

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise enforce session-based access to network devices?
questions.intent: Evaluate session-based network access enforcement

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise enforce session-based access per application per user?
questions.intent: Evaluate per-application session enforcement

questions.prompt: Is session enforcement provided by a centralized authentication or session server rather than only local per-enforcement-point configuration?
questions.intent: Evaluate centralized session enforcement

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise use session renewal automation with re-authentication for network and application access?
questions.intent: Evaluate session renewal with re-authentication

questions.prompt: Are session timeout and renewal policies reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular session policy review

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formally adopt session-based technology like SSO, OIDC, SAML, SSE, or Kerberos?
questions.intent: Evaluate formal SSO and federation adoption
questions.evaluation_focus: Formal adoption of SSO, OIDC, SAML, SSE, or Kerberos

questions.prompt: Are SSO and federation technologies implemented enterprise-wide or for specific applications only?
questions.intent: Evaluate SSO deployment scope

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise integrate SSO and federation with identity stores and access enforcement technology?
questions.intent: Evaluate SSO integration with identity and enforcement
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration with identity and enforcement technology

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise identity store or secrets vault securely manage and store all necessary credentials?
questions.intent: Evaluate secure credential storage in vault or identity store
questions.evaluation_focus: Identity store or vault manages and stores credentials securely

questions.prompt: Does the vault or identity store support automatic provisioning and deprovisioning of credentials?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated credential lifecycle in vault

questions.prompt: Is credential vault configuration reviewed at least quarterly (for example default password policy, certificate timelines, rotation settings)?
questions.intent: Evaluate regular vault configuration review

questions.prompt: Does the credential vault integrate with identity management and authentication systems?
questions.intent: Evaluate vault integration with IAM and authentication
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration with identity management and authentication systems

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have formalized documented processes for credential provisioning, deprovisioning, and secure storage across applications, networks, and endpoints?
questions.intent: Evaluate formalized credential lifecycle processes

questions.prompt: Do credential lifecycle processes apply enterprise-wide or to a specific vertical only?
questions.intent: Evaluate credential lifecycle scope

questions.prompt: Are credentials created, rotated, and revoked automatically based on conditions like new user provisioning, age, or compromise?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated credential lifecycle operations
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated credential creation, rotation, and revocation

questions.prompt: Are service accounts and workloads authenticated using governed methods—not shared human passwords?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether service account and workload authentication is governed
questions.evaluation_focus: Governed service account and workload authentication

questions.prompt: Do API and machine-to-machine clients authenticate using standards such as OAuth 2.0 client credentials, mutual TLS, or certificate-based authentication?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether API authentication uses modern standards
questions.evaluation_focus: OAuth and API client credential authentication standards

questions.prompt: Are shared credentials for non-human identities prohibited and detected through policy or monitoring?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether shared non-human credentials are prohibited
questions.evaluation_focus: Prohibition of shared credentials for non-human identities

questions.prompt: Are service account passwords, API keys, and OAuth client secrets governed with documented lifecycle policies—not ad hoc creation?
questions.intent: Evaluate governed non-human credential lifecycle
questions.evaluation_focus: Governed service account and API credential lifecycle

questions.prompt: Are non-human credentials stored in an enterprise secrets vault rather than plaintext in code, config files, or shared drives?
questions.intent: Evaluate secrets vault storage for non-human credentials
questions.evaluation_focus: Secrets vault storage for non-human credentials

questions.prompt: Is the use of shared human passwords for workloads, service accounts, or automation prohibited by policy?
questions.intent: Evaluate prohibition of shared credentials for non-human identities
questions.evaluation_focus: Prohibition of shared credentials for workloads and automation

questions.prompt: Are API keys and OAuth client secrets rotated automatically on a defined schedule or after compromise?
questions.intent: Evaluate automated rotation of API credentials
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated rotation of API keys and client secrets

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging authentication events—including user lifecycle changes and failed authentications—to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication logging to the SOC is formally documented

questions.prompt: Are authentication events transmitted securely to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether authentication events are logged securely to the SOC
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure authentication event logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are dynamic changes to authentication configuration captured and transferred to the SOC in near-real time?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether dynamic authentication changes are sent to the SOC

questions.prompt: Are anomalous authentication patterns (for example impossible travel, credential stuffing bursts, or token replay) detected and alerted in near-real time?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether anomalous authentication patterns trigger detection and alerting

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging session creation, renewal, and termination to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented session logging to SOC
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented session lifecycle logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are session lifecycle events logged securely to the SOC? (This is distinct from authentication success/failure logs alone.)
questions.intent: Evaluate secure session event logging
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure session event logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Does the SOC detect anomalous session patterns (for example concurrent sessions from disparate locations or impossible travel)?
questions.intent: Evaluate anomaly detection for session events
